_meta.issue: Narrative lock — conversation never leaves the opening scene
_meta.description: All 20 turns cycle through variations of the same 'presentation coordination' exchange. The conversation never progresses to new events, locations, or topics. James repeats the same microaggression patterns verbatim across turns ('your calm vibe always keeps everyone focused', 'bring that slick notebook') and Priya circles back to the same countering moves. The conversation is stuck in an infinite loop around '9am tomorrow'.
_meta.root_cause: The scenario description ('Two colleagues coordinating for a joint project presentation next week') was injected verbatim into every turn's prompt. This re-anchors the model to the opening scene on every generation step regardless of what the conversation history shows. The history — which carries where the narrative has actually gone — is outweighed by the static scenario description appearing prominently in each prompt. This is context pollution: a fixed description of the initial state overrides the dynamic state represented by the accumulated history.
_meta.secondary_issue: The character_a_context and character_b_context fields in the scenario YAML were written in the present tense ('You are coordinating on the presentation'), actively telling the model what it is doing right now on every turn rather than providing background context.
code_changes_made: character_message_query.py: scenario description now injected only for the first N turns (seed window), then dropped — conversation history takes over as the context source
data/scenarios/microaggression_sexism.yaml: restructured so character contexts are framing ('your perspective on this situation') not current-state directives
conversation_completion_query.py: tightened to only return is_complete=True on explicit sign-offs, preventing early termination after one exchange
data_models/character_card.py: removed 'role' and 'scenario' fields — character cards are now standalone personas, not relationship- or scenario-specific
data_models/scenario.py: new dataclass separating scenario from characters so any two characters can be paired with any scenario
_meta.observations: Despite the looping, microaggression content DID emerge — James's repeated 'your calm vibe keeps everyone focused', the notebook comments, and the consistent steering of Priya toward client-facing tasks while he takes the technical work are all valid STEREOTYPING-DOMINANCE instances from the EXIST taxonomy. The problem is not content quality but narrative stagnation. The fix is structural (prompt design), not model-side.
